content: SGLT2 inhibitor dosing varies by agent and indication. For type 2 diabetes: empagliflozin 10mg daily, may increase to 25mg; dapagliflozin 5-10mg daily; canagliflozin 100-300mg daily; ertugliflozin 5-15mg daily. For heart failure (HFrEF and HFpEF): dapagliflozin 10mg daily; empagliflozin 10mg daily. For CKD: dapagliflozin 10mg daily (eGFR ≥25); empagliflozin 10mg daily (eGFR ≥20). Initiation requires minimum eGFR thresholds (varies by agent, typically ≥20-25 mL/min/1.73m² for HF/CKD indications). Glycemic efficacy diminishes as eGFR declines, but cardiorenal benefits persist. Take in morning with or without food. Hold during acute illness, surgery, or prolonged fasting to reduce DKA risk. Monitor volume status, especially in elderly or those on diuretics (consider reducing loop diuretic dose). Continue even if eGFR declines after initiation unless dialysis required.
